prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into exactly one of the following categories: 
alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Category definitions and clarifications:

- alarm_set: Requests to set a simple alarm, timer, or countdown (often for a specific time/duration, e.g., "set alarm for 6 am," "remind me in 10 minutes," "wake me up at..."), not tied to scheduled events or calendar items.
- calendar_set: Requests to schedule or set up an event/reminder for a specific date/time, especially if associated with an event, meeting, or named activity (e.g., "schedule a meeting," "add a dentist appointment," "remind me of my office meeting tomorrow").
    * Tie-break: If "remind" is used with a specific event/activity or future date/time, prefer calendar_set over alarm_set.
    * Use alarm_set only for generic alarms or one-off timed reminders NOT associated with any event/activity.
- play_audiobook: Playing, resuming, or replaying audiobooks specifically (by title, author, or with audiobook terminology). Use if audio content requested is a book, NOT music, podcasts, or radio.
- play_music: Requests to play, resume, or replay music, songs, artists, albums, playlists, or genres. DO NOT use for audiobooks or podcasts.
    * Tie-break: If it's ambiguous between audiobooks and music and the text mentions a book or author, choose play_audiobook.
- general_quirky: Jokes, chitchat, personal questions, or miscellaneous requests not well-suited to other categories.
    * Tie-break: If text asks for facts about a well-known person or thing, but not specific definitions/facts (e.g., "tell me about ...," "has the celebrity ..."), use general_quirky if the intent isn't clear factual Q&A or definition.
- qa_factoid: Direct factual questions (specific, factual answers, e.g., "What is the capital of France?," "How old is Taylor Swift?"). 
- qa_definition: Requests for word meanings/definitions.
- recommendation_locations: Asks for recommendations of places/venues (e.g., "What hiking trail ... is best ...").
- All other categories are as labeled.

If unsure between closely-related categories:
- For "remind me" statements, use calendar_set if associated with any specific event, activity, or scheduled item, otherwise alarm_set.
- For music vs. audiobooks: If text or title can refer to a book, prefer play_audiobook; otherwise play_music.
- For general "tell me about" a person, use general_quirky unless direct factual or definitional Q&A is asked.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into exactly one of the following categories: 
alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Definitions and clarifications:

- alarm_query: Questions about existing alarms (e.g., "Do I have any alarms set?").
- alarm_set: Requests to set, start, or change a simple alarm, timer, or countdown (not tied to an activity/event; generic time/duration or wake-up only).
- calendar_query: Questions about existing calendar events, reminders, or scheduled activities (e.g., "What’s on my calendar today?", "Do I have any meetings tomorrow?").
- calendar_set: Requests to add/set/schedule an event, meeting, appointment, or named reminder for a date/time, or "remind me" for a specific event or activity.
    * Tie-break: If "remind" refers to an event/activity, or names a reason, choose calendar_set. Use alarm_set only for generic timed reminders with no activity/detail.
- calendar_remove: Request to delete/cancel a scheduled calendar entry, event, or reminder.
- cooking_recipe: Requests related to finding or reading out cooking recipes.
- datetime_query: Ask about the date, time, day of week, etc. (e.g., "What's the date today?").
- email_query: Questions about email status, search, or viewing (e.g., "Do I have new emails?", "Read emails from John").
- email_sendemail: Requests to send, write, reply, forward, or edit the address/content of an email.
- general_quirky: Jokes, chitchat, personal questions, or casual requests not suited to other categories. Also use for general "tell me about" or open-ended information requests about people/things when no specific factual or definitional answer is requested.
    * DO NOT use for direct factual, definitional, or clearly classifiable requests.
- iot_hue_lightoff: Requests to turn off Hue/IoT lights.
- lists_createoradd: Requests to create a list or add an item to a list.
- lists_query: Questions about the contents of a list.
- lists_remove: Requests to remove or clear items from a list.
- music_query: Questions about music, albums, artists, or playlist status (e.g., "What’s playing?", "What music do I have by Queen?").
- news_query: Requests for the news or headlines.
- play_audiobook: Requests to play, resume, continue, or replay an audiobook/author/novel. Any mention of audiobook/book/author, or ambiguous "resume playback" where the last action was with audiobooks, choose play_audiobook.
    * Tie-break: If ambiguous between play_music and play_audiobook and book-related terms are present, prefer play_audiobook.
- play_music: Requests to play, resume, continue, or replay music, songs, artists, albums, playlists, or genres. DO NOT use for audiobooks or podcasts.
    * Tie-break: If "resume playback" and last action is unknown and no book-related language, choose play_music.
- play_podcasts: Requests to play or resume podcasts/shows/episodes.
- play_radio: Requests to play or resume radio stations/streams.
- qa_currency: Currency conversions or questions about foreign exchange rates.
- qa_definition: Requests for definitions or meanings of words, terms, or abbreviations. If user asks "what does X mean" or "define X", choose qa_definition even if facts are involved.
- qa_factoid: Direct, factual questions seeking a specific answer (e.g., “What is the capital of France?”, “How old is X?”). Use even for famous people info if a plain fact is requested.
- qa_stock: Questions on stocks, prices, or related financial facts.
- recommendation_events: Request for suggestions of events or things happening (e.g., "What concerts are coming up?").
- recommendation_locations: Request for recommendations of places/venues/attractions/restaurants/trails ("What hiking trails are best?").
    * For requests like "What's the best hiking trail...," use recommendation_locations instead of general_quirky.
- social_post: Requests to post or share content (e.g., "Post this...", "Share update on Facebook").
- takeaway_order: Requests to order or reorder food/takeout.
- transport_query: Questions about transport, navigation, directions, or schedules.
- weather_query: Weather info, forecasts, or weather status.

General tie-breakers and rules:
- If a request could fit more than one category, select the most specific/actionable category from above.
- "Tell me about" + famous person = general_quirky; if it asks for a specific fact, use qa_factoid.
- "Should I go to X" = calendar_query only if it asks about an existing event, else DO NOT assign to recommendation_events—assign to calendar_query if event-related, otherwise general_quirky.
- For ambiguous "resume playback," use context clues (title/author/track/book). If unknown but last request was for audiobook, select play_audiobook; otherwise default to play_music.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
